name: Apple Date Strudel
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the first three ingredients into a bowl.
2. Add the beaten egg and 1/4 cup melted butter to the dry mixture.
3. Gradually add 1/4 cup ice water, stirring until blended.
4. Knead the dough on a lightly floured board until smooth.
5. Cover with a towel and let rest for at least 1/2 hour.
6. Toast the crumb mixture in 2 tbsp butter until lightly browned, then add the nuts and set aside.
7. Peel and core the apples, cutting them into wedges and thin crosswise slices.
8. Mix the apple pieces with the remaining ingredients (dates, sugar, lemon juice, and cinnamon).
9. Roll the dough on a lightly floured board to form a rectangle measuring 24x16 inches.
10. Brush the rolled dough with 2 tbsp butter, melted.
11. Sprinkle the crumb mixture evenly over the dough, stopping about 1 inch from the edges.
12. Spread the apple and date mixture evenly over the crumbs.
13. Roll the strudel tightly from one long side, sealing the ends.
14. Shape the strudel into a horseshoe and place seam-side down on a greased baking sheet.
15. Melt 1/4 cup butter and brush some onto the strudel.
16. Bake in a hot oven at 400°F for about 40 minutes, brushing with additional butter occasionally.
17. Serve warm, sliced.
